Here is a concise, production-quality C# API sketch for a resumable benchmark runner, followed by concurrency tradeoff analysis.

### Code Sketch

```csharp
using System;
using System.Collections.Concurrent;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.IO;
using System.Linq;
using System.Threading;
using System.Threading.Tasks;

public sealed class BenchmarkContext
{
    public required string Repository { get; init; }
    public required string Model { get; init; }
    public required IReadOnlyList<string> Constraints { get; init; }
    
    public double Score(double[] values) => 
        values.Where(double.IsFinite).DefaultIfEmpty(0).Average();
}

public enum RunStatus { Pending, Running, Completed, Failed, Skipped }

public sealed class BenchmarkRun
{
    public BenchmarkContext Context { get; }
    public RunStatus Status { get; set; }
    public double[] RawScores { get; set; } = Array.Empty<double>();
    public double FinalScore => Context.Score(RawScores);
    public string? Error { get; set; }

    public BenchmarkRun(BenchmarkContext ctx) => Context = ctx;
}

public sealed class BenchmarkRunner
{
    private readonly string _stateFile;
    private readonly Func<BenchmarkContext, CancellationToken, Task<double[]>> _executor;
    private readonly ConcurrentDictionary<string, BenchmarkRun> _runs = new();
    private readonly SemaphoreSlim _modelServerLock = new(1, 1); // Single server constraint

    public BenchmarkRunner(string statePath, Func<BenchmarkContext, CancellationToken, Task<double[]>> executor)
    {
        _stateFile = statePath;
        _executor = executor;
    }

    public async Task LoadStateAsync()
    {
